arabic: تَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِي نَزَّلَ ٱلۡفُرۡقَانَ عَلَىٰ عَبۡدِهِۦ لِيَكُونَ لِلۡعَٰلَمِينَ نَذِيرًا
transliteration: Tabaraka allathee nazzala alfurqana AAala AAabdihi liyakoona lilAAalameena natheeran
translation: Blessed is He who has sent down the Criterion [Qur’aan] to His Devotee that he/it may serve as a warner to the worlds.
notes:
Refer study note 1:5 (Chapter 1) for the meaning of ‘worlds’ (AAalameen). The ‘warner’ apparently is the Messenger; but it also could be the Qur’aan.

Refer Verse 21:49 & study note 22:1.

arabic: ٱلَّذِي لَهُۥ مُلۡكُ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَلَمۡ يَتَّخِذۡ وَلَدٗا وَلَمۡ يَكُن لَّهُۥ شَرِيكٞ فِي ٱلۡمُلۡكِ وَخَلَقَ كُلَّ شَيۡءٖ فَقَدَّرَهُۥ تَقۡدِيرٗا
transliteration: Allathee lahu mulku alssamawati waalardi walam yattakhith waladan walam yakun lahu shareekun fee almulki wakhalaqa kulla shayin faqaddarahu taqdeeran
translation: Blessed is He to Whom belongs the the absolute sovereignty over the heavens and the earth, He Who has begotten no children and has no partner in His sovereignty. It is He Who has created everything and it is He Who has given it its role.
notes: Guardians/protectors. For a more comprehensive meaning of awliya, refer study note 2:154 (Manzil I).

arabic: وَٱتَّخَذُواْ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ ءَالِهَةٗ لَّا يَخۡلُقُونَ شَيۡـٔٗا وَهُمۡ يُخۡلَقُونَ وَلَا يَمۡلِكُونَ لِأَنفُسِهِمۡ ضَرّٗا وَلَا نَفۡعٗا وَلَا يَمۡلِكُونَ مَوۡتٗا وَلَا حَيَوٰةٗ وَلَا نُشُورٗا
transliteration: Waittakhathoo min doonihi alihatan la yakhluqoona shayan wahum yukhlaqoona wala yamlikoona lianfusihim darran wala nafAAan wala yamlikoona mawtan wala hayatan wala nushooran
translation: And yet they have taken, besides Him, deities who create nothing and they themselves are created, and who have no power to harm or benefit themselves and who who have no power over death, life, or Resurrection.
notes: Defacto sovereignty in the entire universe, including the earth, has always been Allah’s, but He has made mankind His vicegerents on earth for a temporary period. This vicegerency too shall be taken away from mankind on the Last Day.

arabic: وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓاْ إِنۡ هَٰذَآ إِلَّآ إِفۡكٌ ٱفۡتَرَىٰهُ وَأَعَانَهُۥ عَلَيۡهِ قَوۡمٌ ءَاخَرُونَۖ فَقَدۡ جَآءُو ظُلۡمٗا وَزُورٗا
transliteration: Waqala allatheena kafaroo in hatha illa ifkun iftarahu waaAAanahu AAalayhi qawmun akharoona faqad jaoo thulman wazooran
translation: Those who suppress the Truth say, “This is only a self- concocted lie and others have helped him in concocting it.” Verily, then, they have perpetrated an injustice and a falsehood.
notes: The Messenger (Muhammad, peace on him) might have said so, exasperatingly, when in the initial period of his mission the people of his own tribe would not believe him. But from the immediately preceding context it appears that this is what he would say on Judgment Day when he comes to know of the sorry fate of a large number of people who claimed to be his followers after he left this world. Have not most of the so-called Muslims of today (early part of the 21st century A.D.) indeed abandoned the Qur’aan? Do they ever refer to it seeking guidance in the conduct of their day- to-day lives in this world? The Qur’aan urges them to stand by and abide by the Truth with patience and with trust in Allah. Do they do that? At the slightest perception of danger to any of their worldly assets, they will not hesitate to abandon the truth and thereby the Qur’aan. The Qur’aan asks them to worship none but Allah. But they run to the graves of dead saints for redressal of grievances and for bestowal of bounties. They fail to understand that such acts of theirs are tantamount to worshipping the dead saints and thereby committing the unpardonable sin of shirk. The Qur’aan asks them to believe in the Hereafter. They do profess that they believe, but they betray their disbelief in almost every act of theirs in this world. They would rather abandon their morning prayer than abandon their sweet sleep. They do not bother to study for themselves what the Qur’aan says. They will try their best to educate their children to earn a lot and to have a good status in worldly life, but they bother the least to prepare them for the Hereafter. They do many, many such things to prove the prophetic statement in this Verse right.

arabic: وَقَالُوٓاْ أَسَٰطِيرُ ٱلۡأَوَّلِينَ ٱكۡتَتَبَهَا فَهِيَ تُمۡلَىٰ عَلَيۡهِ بُكۡرَةٗ وَأَصِيلٗا
transliteration: Waqaloo asateeru alawwaleena iktatabaha fahiya tumla AAalayhi bukratan waaseelan
translation: And they say, “Fables of the ancients! He has had these written down which then are rehearsed to him morning and evening.”
notes: Verse 97:1 says that the Qur’aan was sent down in the Night of Power. This indicates that the divine book was in fact issued all at once; but it was revealed to the Prophet little by little, as the occasion arose, during the last 23 years of his life. And, so, through the Prophet’s conscious mind the Qur’aan became public little by little.

arabic: قُلۡ أَنزَلَهُ ٱلَّذِي يَعۡلَمُ ٱلسِّرَّ فِي ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ غَفُورٗا رَّحِيمٗا
transliteration: Qul anzalahu allathee yaAAlamu alssirra fee alssamawati waalardi innahu kana ghafooran raheeman
translation: Say, “HE has sent it down Who knows every secret that is in the heavens and on the earth. He is indeed Forgiving, Merciful.”
notes: Peoples of pre-historic ages. This Verse is in continuation of the previous one implying transgression of the peoples mentioned here also and of their punishment in the Hereafter.

arabic: وَقَالُواْ مَالِ هَٰذَا ٱلرَّسُولِ يَأۡكُلُ ٱلطَّعَامَ وَيَمۡشِي فِي ٱلۡأَسۡوَاقِ لَوۡلَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيۡهِ مَلَكٞ فَيَكُونَ مَعَهُۥ نَذِيرًا
transliteration: Waqaloo mali hatha alrrasooli yakulu alttaAAama wayamshee fee alaswaqi lawla onzila ilayhi malakun fayakoona maAAahu natheeran
translation: And they say, “What kind of a Messenger is this who eats food and roams in the marketplaces!? Why has no angel been sent down with him to warn us?”
notes: I do not know about those Arabs at the time of the revelation of the Qur’aan; but in my time now (early part of 21st century A.D.) people all the world over have come to see the ruins of the towns inhabited by the people of Lot guilty of the sin of homosexuality and punished therefor. Click the video to see these for yourselves from the comforts of your homes. These YouTube videos provide yet another proof that the Qur’aan – as also the original scriptures revealed before – is genuinely divine. Yet most people even now would not believe in the divine assertion that mankind is going to be resurrected to face the consequence of their disbelief and of their committing sins like homosexuality.

arabic: أَوۡ يُلۡقَىٰٓ إِلَيۡهِ كَنزٌ أَوۡ تَكُونُ لَهُۥ جَنَّةٞ يَأۡكُلُ مِنۡهَاۚ وَقَالَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ إِن تَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا رَجُلٗا مَّسۡحُورًا
transliteration: Aw yulqa ilayhi kanzun aw takoonu lahu jannatun yakulu minha waqala alththalimoona in tattabiAAoona illa rajulan mashooran
translation: “Or no treasure been sent down to him, or no garden been provided to him from which to eat?” The wrongdoers further say, “You are not following but a man bewitched!”
notes: Reference in these two Verses 45 and 46 is obviously to the lengthening of the shadows in the evenings and their gradual contraction in the mornings. And the fact that this natural phenomenon is essential is accentuated by the remark that Allah could, if He so willed, make the shadows stationary. No life would have been possible on the earth in that case! In the metaphorical sense, moreover, like the sun guiding the shadows on earth, Allah Almighty is closely monitoring the trials & tribulations (shadows) in human life and can easily withdraw these. A human being has just to abide by the divine law and put his/her trust in Allah.

arabic: ٱنظُرۡ كَيۡفَ ضَرَبُواْ لَكَ ٱلۡأَمۡثَٰلَ فَضَلُّواْ فَلَا يَسۡتَطِيعُونَ سَبِيلٗا
transliteration: Onthur kayfa daraboo laka alamthala fadalloo fala yastateeAAoona sabeelan
translation: See what kind of attributes they expect you to have. They have thus gone astray and cannot find the right way.
notes: The natural facility described in this Verse serves also as a metaphor for death and resurrection of human life.

arabic: تَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِيٓ إِن شَآءَ جَعَلَ لَكَ خَيۡرٗا مِّن ذَٰلِكَ جَنَّـٰتٖ تَجۡرِي مِن تَحۡتِهَا ٱلۡأَنۡهَٰرُ وَيَجۡعَل لَّكَ قُصُورَۢا
transliteration: Tabaraka allathee in shaa jaAAala laka khayran min thalika jannatin tajree min tahtiha alanharu wayajAAal laka qusooran
translation: Blessed is He who can give you, if He wills, better things than that: gardens under which rivers flow. And He can provide palaces for you.
notes: At the mouth of River Amazon (South America) the ocean has fresh water extending so far out thereinto that the land cannot be seen from there. The ocean beyond this fresh water area is salty. This Verse here obviously refers to such huge bodies of water meeting each other without influenceing the character of either. This is the only place where such a thing spectacularly happens anywhere in the world. And at the time when the Qur’aan was revealed, the Americas were unknown to the rest of the world. And yet it is mentioned in the Qur’aan. This provides yet another proof that the Book is divine. Following is the relevant extract from Wikipedia: The quantity of water released by the Amazon to the Atlantic Ocean is enormous: up to 300,000 cubic metres per second (11,000,000 cu ft/s) in the rainy season, with an average of 209,000 cubic metres per second (7,400,000 cu ft/s) from 1973 to 1990. The Amazon is responsible for about 20% of the Earth's fresh water entering the ocean. The river pushes a vast plume of fresh water into the ocean. The plume is about 400 kilometres (250 mi) long and between 100 and 200 kilometres (62 and 120 mi) wide. The fresh water, being lighter, overrides the salty ocean, diluting the salinity and altering the color of the ocean surface over an area up to 1,000,000 square miles (2,600,000 km2) large. For centuries ships have reported fresh water near the Amazon's mouth yet well out of sight of land in what otherwise seemed to be the open ocean. [Source]

arabic: بَلۡ كَذَّبُواْ بِٱلسَّاعَةِۖ وَأَعۡتَدۡنَا لِمَن كَذَّبَ بِٱلسَّاعَةِ سَعِيرًا
transliteration: Bal kaththaboo bialssaAAati waaAAtadna liman kaththaba bialssaAAati saAAeeran
translation: But they deny the Hour . We have prepared a blazing Fire for those who deny the Hour.
notes:
Refer Verse 21:49 & study note 22:1.

There is a distinct divine prompting here for the believers to ponder over the natural phenomenon of the day and the night. Some Muslims with a scientific bend of mind did ponder and were successful in discovering that the earth was not stationary as people believed till then. Their pioneering discoveries led to the discovery of the real cause behind the occurrence of day and night. [Source] And for the Muslims in general the phenomenon is something to be very grateful about to their Lord; for, without it, it would be impossible to live on earth.

arabic: إِذَا رَأَتۡهُم مِّن مَّكَانِۭ بَعِيدٖ سَمِعُواْ لَهَا تَغَيُّظٗا وَزَفِيرٗا
transliteration: Itha raathum min makanin baAAeedin samiAAoo laha taghayyuthan wazafeeran
translation: When it (the Fire) sees them from afar, they will hear it raging and roaring.
notes: My understanding of this Verse is that the Lord wants the righteous people not to do anything wrong, improper, unlawful or illegal under cover of darkness of the night. HE wants no wrong done during the day too, but the night provides more temptations for wrong-doing, and hence the emphasis thereon.

arabic: وَإِذَآ أُلۡقُواْ مِنۡهَا مَكَانٗا ضَيِّقٗا مُّقَرَّنِينَ دَعَوۡاْ هُنَالِكَ ثُبُورٗا
transliteration: Waitha olqoo minha makanan dayyiqan muqarraneena daAAaw hunalika thubooran
translation: When they are thrown into a narrow space therein, chained together, they will plead for destruction (death, so that they become oblivious to their suffering in the Fire).
notes: La tadAAoo alyawma thubooran wahidan waodAAoo thubooran katheeran

arabic: لَّا تَدۡعُواْ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ ثُبُورٗا وَٰحِدٗا وَٱدۡعُواْ ثُبُورٗا كَثِيرٗا
transliteration: La tadAAoo alyawma thubooran wahidan waodAAoo thubooran katheeran
translation: “Plead not this day for a single destruction but plead for many!”
notes: Qul athalika khayrun am jannatu alkhuldi allatee wuAAida almuttaqoona kanat lahum jazaan wamaseeran

arabic: قُلۡ أَذَٰلِكَ خَيۡرٌ أَمۡ جَنَّةُ ٱلۡخُلۡدِ ٱلَّتِي وُعِدَ ٱلۡمُتَّقُونَۚ كَانَتۡ لَهُمۡ جَزَآءٗ وَمَصِيرٗا
transliteration: Qul athalika khayrun am jannatu alkhuldi allatee wuAAida almuttaqoona kanat lahum jazaan wamaseeran
translation: Ask, “Is that better, or the Paradise of immortality which the pious/righteous have been promised? It is their reward and their destination.”
notes: Lahum feeha ma yashaoona khalideena kana AAala rabbika waAAdan mas-oolan

arabic: لَّهُمۡ فِيهَا مَا يَشَآءُونَ خَٰلِدِينَۚ كَانَ عَلَىٰ رَبِّكَ وَعۡدٗا مَّسۡـُٔولٗا
transliteration: Lahum feeha ma yashaoona khalideena kana AAala rabbika waAAdan masoolan
translation: They shall forever have therein for them all that they desire. This is a promise frequently asked for of your Lord.
notes: Wayawma yahshuruhum wama yaAAbudoona min dooni Allahi fayaqoolu aantum adlaltum AAibadee haola-i am hum dalloo alssabeela

arabic: وَيَوۡمَ يَحۡشُرُهُمۡ وَمَا يَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ فَيَقُولُ ءَأَنتُمۡ أَضۡلَلۡتُمۡ عِبَادِي هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ أَمۡ هُمۡ ضَلُّواْ ٱلسَّبِيلَ
transliteration: Wayawma yahshuruhum wama yaAAbudoona min dooni Allahi fayaqoolu aantum adlaltum AAibadee haolai am hum dalloo alssabeela
translation: On the Day He gathers them together with those they worship besides Him, He will ask, “Did you mislead these people whom I had created to worship Me, or did they stray away by themselves?”
notes: Qaloo subhanaka ma kana yanbaghee lana an nattakhitha min doonika min awliyaa walakin mattaAAtahum waabaahum hatta nasoo alththikra wakanoo qawman booran

arabic: قَالُواْ سُبۡحَٰنَكَ مَا كَانَ يَنۢبَغِي لَنَآ أَن نَّتَّخِذَ مِن دُونِكَ مِنۡ أَوۡلِيَآءَ وَلَٰكِن مَّتَّعۡتَهُمۡ وَءَابَآءَهُمۡ حَتَّىٰ نَسُواْ ٱلذِّكۡرَ وَكَانُواْ قَوۡمَۢا بُورٗا
transliteration: Qaloo subhanaka ma kana yanbaghee lana an nattakhitha min doonika min awliyaa walakin mattaAAtahum waabaahum hatta nasoo alththikra wakanoo qawman booran
translation: They will say, “Your Holiness! It was not proper for us to choose any of the awliya other than You. But You gave them and their fathers the comforts of this life so much that they forgot the Remembrance (of You and Your Message, the Qur’aan) and thus became a people doomed.”
notes:
Guardians/protectors. For a more comprehensive meaning of awliya, refer study note 2:154 (Manzil I).

Faqad kaththabookum bima taqooloona fama tastateeAAoona sarfan wala nasran waman yathlim minkum nuthiqhu AAathaban

arabic: فَقَدۡ كَذَّبُوكُم بِمَا تَقُولُونَ فَمَا تَسۡتَطِيعُونَ صَرۡفٗا وَلَا نَصۡرٗاۚ وَمَن يَظۡلِم مِّنكُمۡ نُذِقۡهُ عَذَابٗا كَبِيرٗا
transliteration: Faqad kaththabookum bima taqooloona fama tastateeAAoona sarfan wala nasran waman yathlim minkum nuthiqhu AAathaban kabeeran
translation: They have certainly then given the lie to all your pleas and you can neither ward off punishment nor get any help. And whoever of you has committed evil, he/she shall be made by Us to taste great punishment!
notes: Wama arsalna qablaka mina almursaleena illa innahum laya/kuloona alttaAAama wayamshoona fee al-aswaqi wajaAAalna baAAdakum libaAAdin fitnatan atasbiroona wakana rabbuka baseeran

arabic: وَمَآ أَرۡسَلۡنَا قَبۡلَكَ مِنَ ٱلۡمُرۡسَلِينَ إِلَّآ إِنَّهُمۡ لَيَأۡكُلُونَ ٱلطَّعَامَ وَيَمۡشُونَ فِي ٱلۡأَسۡوَاقِۗ وَجَعَلۡنَا بَعۡضَكُمۡ لِبَعۡضٖ فِتۡنَةً أَتَصۡبِرُونَۗ وَكَانَ رَبُّكَ بَصِيرٗا
transliteration: Wama arsalna qablaka mina almursaleena illa innahum layakuloona alttaAAama wayamshoona fee alaswaqi wajaAAalna baAAdakum libaAAdin fitnatan atasbiroona wakana rabbuka baseeran
translation: We had not sent any Messengers before you who did not eat food and roam the market-places. We test some of you with others, whether you exercise patience. And your Lord monitors all things.
notes: Waqala allatheena la yarjoona liqaana lawla onzila AAalayna almala-ikatu aw nara rabbana laqadi istakbaroo fee anfusihim waAAataw AAutuwwan kabeeran

arabic: ۞وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَرۡجُونَ لِقَآءَنَا لَوۡلَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيۡنَا ٱلۡمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ أَوۡ نَرَىٰ رَبَّنَاۗ لَقَدِ ٱسۡتَكۡبَرُواْ فِيٓ أَنفُسِهِمۡ وَعَتَوۡ عُتُوّٗا كَبِيرٗا
transliteration: Waqala allatheena la yarjoona liqaana lawla onzila AAalayna almalaikatu aw nara rabbana laqadi istakbaroo fee anfusihim waAAataw AAutuwwan kabeeran
translation: And those who do not expect a meeting with Us say, “Why are angels not sent down to us or why do we not see our Lord?” They are certainly too proud of themselves and have greatly exceeded all bounds.
notes: Yawma yarawna almala-ikata la bushra yawma-ithin lilmujrimeena wayaqooloona hijran mahjooran

arabic: يَوۡمَ يَرَوۡنَ ٱلۡمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ لَا بُشۡرَىٰ يَوۡمَئِذٖ لِّلۡمُجۡرِمِينَ وَيَقُولُونَ حِجۡرٗا مَّحۡجُورٗا
transliteration: Yawma yarawna almalaikata la bushra yawmaithin lilmujrimeena wayaqooloona hijran mahjooran
translation: There will be no good tidings for the guilty on the day they see the angels. And they will cry out for an invincible partition between them and the angels!
notes: Waqadimna ila ma AAamiloo min AAamalin fajaAAalnahu habaan manthooran

arabic: وَقَدِمۡنَآ إِلَىٰ مَا عَمِلُواْ مِنۡ عَمَلٖ فَجَعَلۡنَٰهُ هَبَآءٗ مَّنثُورًا
transliteration: Waqadimna ila ma AAamiloo min AAamalin fajaAAalnahu habaan manthooran
translation: And We shall proceed to turn all that they did into scattered dust.
notes: As-habu aljannati yawma-ithin khayrun mustaqarran waahsanu maqeelan

arabic: أَصۡحَٰبُ ٱلۡجَنَّةِ يَوۡمَئِذٍ خَيۡرٞ مُّسۡتَقَرّٗا وَأَحۡسَنُ مَقِيلٗا
transliteration: Ashabu aljannati yawmaithin khayrun mustaqarran waahsanu maqeelan
translation: The inhabitants of the Garden will have the best abode and the finest place of rest on that Day.
notes: Wayawma tashaqqaqu alssamao bialghamami wanuzzila almala-ikatu tanzeelan

arabic: وَيَوۡمَ تَشَقَّقُ ٱلسَّمَآءُ بِٱلۡغَمَٰمِ وَنُزِّلَ ٱلۡمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ تَنزِيلًا
transliteration: Wayawma tashaqqaqu alssamao bialghamami wanuzzila almalaikatu tanzeelan
translation: On a Day when the sky will split open with its clouds and the angels after angels are sent down,
notes: Almulku yawma-ithin alhaqqu lilrrahmani wakana yawman AAala alkafireena AAaseeran

arabic: ٱلۡمُلۡكُ يَوۡمَئِذٍ ٱلۡحَقُّ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِۚ وَكَانَ يَوۡمًا عَلَى ٱلۡكَٰفِرِينَ عَسِيرٗا
transliteration: Almulku yawmaithin alhaqqu lilrrahmani wakana yawman AAala alkafireena AAaseeran
translation: Sovereignty on that Day shall return defacto and dejure to the Gracious One. And it will be a hard Day for those who suppress the Truth.
notes:
Defacto sovereignty in the entire universe, including the earth, has always been Allah’s, but He has made mankind His vicegerents on earth for a temporary period. This vicegerency too shall be taken away from mankind on the Last Day.

Wayawma yaAAaddu alththalimu AAala yadayhi yaqoolu ya laytanee ittakhathtu maAAa alrrasooli sabeelan

arabic: وَيَوۡمَ يَعَضُّ ٱلظَّالِمُ عَلَىٰ يَدَيۡهِ يَقُولُ يَٰلَيۡتَنِي ٱتَّخَذۡتُ مَعَ ٱلرَّسُولِ سَبِيلٗا
transliteration: Wayawma yaAAaddu alththalimu AAala yadayhi yaqoolu ya laytanee ittakhathtu maAAa alrrasooli sabeelan
translation: On that Day, the transgressor will bite his hands and say, “Would that I had walked the Messenger’s path!”
notes: Ya waylata laytanee lam attakhith fulanan khaleelan

arabic: يَٰوَيۡلَتَىٰ لَيۡتَنِي لَمۡ أَتَّخِذۡ فُلَانًا خَلِيلٗا
transliteration: Ya waylata laytanee lam attakhith fulanan khaleelan
translation: “Oh would that I had never chosen such-and-such as my friend!”
notes: Laqad adallanee AAani alththikri baAAda ith jaanee wakana alshshaytanu lil-insani khathoolan

arabic: لَّقَدۡ أَضَلَّنِي عَنِ ٱلذِّكۡرِ بَعۡدَ إِذۡ جَآءَنِيۗ وَكَانَ ٱلشَّيۡطَٰنُ لِلۡإِنسَٰنِ خَذُولٗا
transliteration: Laqad adallanee AAani alththikri baAAda ith jaanee wakana alshshaytanu lilinsani khathoolan
translation: “He made me stray away from Your Message after it had reached me. And the Satan is betrayer of mankind.”
notes: Waqala alrrasoolu ya rabbi inna qawmee ittakhathoo hatha alqur-ana mahjooran

arabic: وَقَالَ ٱلرَّسُولُ يَٰرَبِّ إِنَّ قَوۡمِي ٱتَّخَذُواْ هَٰذَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ مَهۡجُورٗا
transliteration: Waqala alrrasoolu ya rabbi inna qawmee ittakhathoo hatha alqurana mahjooran
translation: And the Messenger will say, “O my Lord! My people did indeed treat this Qur’aan as a thing abandoned.”
notes:
The Messenger (Muhammad, peace on him) might have said so, exasperatingly, when in the initial period of his mission the people of his own tribe would not believe him. But from the immediately preceding context it appears that this is what he would say on Judgment Day when he comes to know of the sorry fate of a large number of people who claimed to be his followers after he left this world. Have not most of the so-called Muslims of today (early part of the 21st century A.D.) indeed abandoned the Qur’aan? Do they ever refer to it seeking guidance in the conduct of their day- to-day lives in this world? The Qur’aan urges them to stand by and abide by the Truth with patience and with trust in Allah. Do they do that? At the slightest perception of danger to any of their worldly assets, they will not hesitate to abandon the truth and thereby the Qur’aan. The Qur’aan asks them to worship none but Allah. But they run to the graves of dead saints for redressal of grievances and for bestowal of bounties. They fail to understand that such acts of theirs are tantamount to worshipping the dead saints and thereby committing the unpardonable sin of shirk. The Qur’aan asks them to believe in the Hereafter. They do profess that they believe, but they betray their disbelief in almost every act of theirs in this world. They would rather abandon their morning prayer than abandon their sweet sleep. They do not bother to study for themselves what the Qur’aan says. They will try their best to educate their children to earn a lot and to have a good status in worldly life, but they bother the least to prepare them for the Hereafter. They do many, many such things to prove the prophetic statement in this Verse right.

Wakathalika jaAAalna likulli nabiyyin AAaduwwan mina almujrimeena wakafa birabbika hadiyan wanaseeran

arabic: وَكَذَٰلِكَ جَعَلۡنَا لِكُلِّ نَبِيٍّ عَدُوّٗا مِّنَ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِينَۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِرَبِّكَ هَادِيٗا وَنَصِيرٗا
transliteration: Wakathalika jaAAalna likulli nabiyyin AAaduwwan mina almujrimeena wakafa birabbika hadiyan wanaseeran
translation: And thus did We make to every prophet an inimical front from among the sinners; but your Lord is sufficient as Guide and Support.
notes: Waqala allatheena kafaroo lawla nuzzila AAalayhi alqur-anu jumlatan wahidatan kathalika linuthabbita bihi fu- adaka warattalnahu tarteelan

arabic: وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ لَوۡلَا نُزِّلَ عَلَيۡهِ ٱلۡقُرۡءَانُ جُمۡلَةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗۚ كَذَٰلِكَ لِنُثَبِّتَ بِهِۦ فُؤَادَكَۖ وَرَتَّلۡنَٰهُ تَرۡتِيلٗا
transliteration: Waqala allatheena kafaroo lawla nuzzila AAalayhi alquranu jumlatan wahidatan kathalika linuthabbita bihi fuadaka warattalnahu tarteelan
translation: Those who suppress the Truth say, “Why was the Qur’aan not sent down to him all at once?” We did send it down thus so that We imbue your mind therewith. And We have arranged it in good order.
notes:
Verse 97:1 says that the Qur’aan was sent down in the Night of Power. This indicates that the divine book was in fact issued all at once; but it was revealed to the Prophet little by little, as the occasion arose, during the last 23 years of his life. And, so, through the Prophet’s conscious mind the Qur’aan became public little by little.

Wala ya/toonaka bimathalin illa ji/naka bialhaqqi waahsana tafseeran

arabic: وَلَا يَأۡتُونَكَ بِمَثَلٍ إِلَّا جِئۡنَٰكَ بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَأَحۡسَنَ تَفۡسِيرًا
transliteration: Wala yatoonaka bimathalin illa jinaka bialhaqqi waahsana tafseeran
translation: No sooner do they come up with a problem than We bring you the truth behind and the best solution.
notes: Allatheena yuhsharoona AAala wujoohihim ila jahannama ola-ika sharrun makanan waadallu sabeelan

arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يُحۡشَرُونَ عَلَىٰ وُجُوهِهِمۡ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ أُوْلَـٰٓئِكَ شَرّٞ مَّكَانٗا وَأَضَلُّ سَبِيلٗا
transliteration: Allatheena yuhsharoona AAala wujoohihim ila jahannama olaika sharrun makanan waadallu sabeelan
translation: Those who will be dragged headlong into Hell shall have an evil place to dwell in – and they had strayed away from the Right Path.
notes: Walaqad atayna moosa alkitaba wajaAAalna maAAahu akhahu haroona wazeeran

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ ءَاتَيۡنَا مُوسَى ٱلۡكِتَٰبَ وَجَعَلۡنَا مَعَهُۥٓ أَخَاهُ هَٰرُونَ وَزِيرٗا
transliteration: Walaqad atayna moosa alkitaba wajaAAalna maAAahu akhahu haroona wazeeran
translation: And We did give Moses the Book and made his brother Aaron his assistant.
notes: Faqulna ithhaba ila alqawmi allatheena kaththaboo bi-ayatina fadammarnahum tadmeeran

arabic: فَقُلۡنَا ٱذۡهَبَآ إِلَى ٱلۡقَوۡمِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُواْ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَا فَدَمَّرۡنَٰهُمۡ تَدۡمِيرٗا
transliteration: Faqulna ithhaba ila alqawmi allatheena kaththaboo biayatina fadammarnahum tadmeeran
translation: We then told them both to go to the people who had denied Our signs. We then put those people to ruin!
notes: Waqawma noohin lamma kaththaboo alrrusula aghraqnahum wajaAAalnahum lilnnasi ayatan waaAAtadna lilththalimeena AAathaban aleeman

arabic: وَقَوۡمَ نُوحٖ لَّمَّا كَذَّبُواْ ٱلرُّسُلَ أَغۡرَقۡنَٰهُمۡ وَجَعَلۡنَٰهُمۡ لِلنَّاسِ ءَايَةٗۖ وَأَعۡتَدۡنَا لِلظَّـٰلِمِينَ عَذَابًا أَلِيمٗا
transliteration: Waqawma noohin lamma kaththaboo alrrusula aghraqnahum wajaAAalnahum lilnnasi ayatan waaAAtadna lilththalimeena AAathaban aleeman
translation: And We drowned the people of Noah when they rejected the Messengers and We made an example of them (the people) for all mankind. And We have prepared a painful punishment for the transgressors!
notes: WaAAadan wathamooda waas-haba alrrassi waquroonan bayna thalika katheeran

arabic: وَعَادٗا وَثَمُودَاْ وَأَصۡحَٰبَ ٱلرَّسِّ وَقُرُونَۢا بَيۡنَ ذَٰلِكَ كَثِيرٗا
transliteration: WaAAadan wathamooda waashaba alrrassi waquroonan bayna thalika katheeran
translation: For AAad , Thamood , and the people of al-Rass , and for many a generation between them.
notes:
Peoples of pre-historic ages. This Verse is in continuation of the previous one implying transgression of the peoples mentioned here also and of their punishment in the Hereafter.

Wakullan darabna lahu al-amthala wakullan tabbarna tatbeeran

arabic: وَكُلّٗا ضَرَبۡنَا لَهُ ٱلۡأَمۡثَٰلَۖ وَكُلّٗا تَبَّرۡنَا تَتۡبِيرٗا
transliteration: Wakullan darabna lahu alamthala wakullan tabbarna tatbeeran
translation: To every one of them We gave warning examples and every one of them We put to annihilation.
notes: Walaqad ataw AAala alqaryati allatee omtirat matara alssaw-i afalam yakoonoo yarawnaha bal kanoo la yarjoona nushooran

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ أَتَوۡاْ عَلَى ٱلۡقَرۡيَةِ ٱلَّتِيٓ أُمۡطِرَتۡ مَطَرَ ٱلسَّوۡءِۚ أَفَلَمۡ يَكُونُواْ يَرَوۡنَهَاۚ بَلۡ كَانُواْ لَا يَرۡجُونَ نُشُورٗا
transliteration: Walaqad ataw AAala alqaryati allatee omtirat matara alssawi afalam yakoonoo yarawnaha bal kanoo la yarjoona nushooran
translation: And they must have certainly come upon the town/village on which had rained down an evil rain. How could they not have seen it? Yet they have no faith in the Resurrection.
notes:
I do not know about those Arabs at the time of the revelation of the Qur’aan; but in my time now (early part of 21st century A.D.) people all the world over have come to see the ruins of the towns inhabited by the people of Lot guilty of the sin of homosexuality and punished therefor. Click the video to see these for yourselves from the comforts of your homes. These YouTube videos provide yet another proof that the Qur’aan – as also the original scriptures revealed before – is genuinely divine. Yet most people even now would not believe in the divine assertion that mankind is going to be resurrected to face the consequence of their disbelief and of their committing sins like homosexuality.

Wa-itha raawka in yattakhithoonaka illa huzuwan ahatha allathee baAAatha Allahu rasoolan

arabic: وَإِذَا رَأَوۡكَ إِن يَتَّخِذُونَكَ إِلَّا هُزُوًا أَهَٰذَا ٱلَّذِي بَعَثَ ٱللَّهُ رَسُولًا
transliteration: Waitha raawka in yattakhithoonaka illa huzuwan ahatha allathee baAAatha Allahu rasoolan
translation: Whenever they see you, they hold you not but in contempt, saying, “Is this the one Allah has sent as His Messenger?”
notes: In kada layudilluna AAan alihatina lawla an sabarna AAalayha wasawfa yaAAlamoona heena yarawna alAAathaba man adallu sabeelan

arabic: إِن كَادَ لَيُضِلُّنَا عَنۡ ءَالِهَتِنَا لَوۡلَآ أَن صَبَرۡنَا عَلَيۡهَاۚ وَسَوۡفَ يَعۡلَمُونَ حِينَ يَرَوۡنَ ٱلۡعَذَابَ مَنۡ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا
transliteration: In kada layudilluna AAan alihatina lawla an sabarna AAalayha wasawfa yaAAlamoona heena yarawna alAAathaba man adallu sabeelan
translation: “Indeed, he would almost have led us away from our deities, had we not stood firmly with them!” And when they experience the punishment, they shall realize who it is that had strayed from the path.
notes: Araayta mani ittakhatha ilahahu hawahu afaanta takoonu AAalayhi wakeelan

arabic: أَرَءَيۡتَ مَنِ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَٰهَهُۥ هَوَىٰهُ أَفَأَنتَ تَكُونُ عَلَيۡهِ وَكِيلًا
transliteration: Araayta mani ittakhatha ilahahu hawahu afaanta takoonu AAalayhi wakeelan
translation: Have you seen one who has taken one’s own desire to be one’a god? Can you be a guardian over such a one?
notes: Am tahsabu anna aktharahum yasmaAAoona aw yaAAqiloona in hum illa kaal-anAAami bal hum adallu sabeelan

arabic: أَمۡ تَحۡسَبُ أَنَّ أَكۡثَرَهُمۡ يَسۡمَعُونَ أَوۡ يَعۡقِلُونَۚ إِنۡ هُمۡ إِلَّا كَٱلۡأَنۡعَٰمِ بَلۡ هُمۡ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا
transliteration: Am tahsabu anna aktharahum yasmaAAoona aw yaAAqiloona in hum illa kaalanAAami bal hum adallu sabeelan
translation: Do you think most of them can hear or understand? They are nothing but like cattle. Nay, they are those astray from the Path.
notes: Alam tara ila rabbika kayfa madda alththilla walaw shaa lajaAAalahu sakinan thumma jaAAalna alshshamsa AAalayhi daleelan

arabic: أَلَمۡ تَرَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ كَيۡفَ مَدَّ ٱلظِّلَّ وَلَوۡ شَآءَ لَجَعَلَهُۥ سَاكِنٗا ثُمَّ جَعَلۡنَا ٱلشَّمۡسَ عَلَيۡهِ دَلِيلٗا
transliteration: Alam tara ila rabbika kayfa madda alththilla walaw shaa lajaAAalahu sakinan thumma jaAAalna alshshamsa AAalayhi daleelan
translation: Have you not observed how your Lord stretches the shadow? Had He pleased, He could have made it stationary. And We made the sun a guide over it.
notes: Thumma qabadnahu ilayna qabdan yaseeran

arabic: ثُمَّ قَبَضۡنَٰهُ إِلَيۡنَا قَبۡضٗا يَسِيرٗا
transliteration: Thumma qabadnahu ilayna qabdan yaseeran
translation: Then We withdraw it (shadow) to Us, an easy withdrawal.
notes:
Reference in these two Verses 45 and 46 is obviously to the lengthening of the shadows in the evenings and their gradual contraction in the mornings. And the fact that this natural phenomenon is essential is accentuated by the remark that Allah could, if He so willed, make the shadows stationary. No life would have been possible on the earth in that case! In the metaphorical sense, moreover, like the sun guiding the shadows on earth, Allah Almighty is closely monitoring the trials & tribulations (shadows) in human life and can easily withdraw these. A human being has just to abide by the divine law and put his/her trust in Allah.

Wahuwa allathee jaAAala lakumu allayla libasan waalnnawma subatan wajaAAala alnnahara nushooran

arabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلَّيۡلَ لِبَاسٗا وَٱلنَّوۡمَ سُبَاتٗا وَجَعَلَ ٱلنَّهَارَ نُشُورٗا
transliteration: Wahuwa allathee jaAAala lakumu allayla libasan waalnnawma subatan wajaAAala alnnahara nushooran
translation: And it is He Who has made for you the night as mantle, the sleep for repose, and the day for rising.
notes:
The natural facility described in this Verse serves also as a metaphor for death and resurrection of human life.

Wahuwa allathee arsala alrriyaha bushran bayna yaday rahmatihi waanzalna mina alssama-i maan tahooran

arabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِيٓ أَرۡسَلَ ٱلرِّيَٰحَ بُشۡرَۢا بَيۡنَ يَدَيۡ رَحۡمَتِهِۦۚ وَأَنزَلۡنَا مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءٗ طَهُورٗا
transliteration: Wahuwa allathee arsala alrriyaha bushran bayna yaday rahmatihi waanzalna mina alssamai maan tahooran
translation: It is He who sends the winds as heralds bearing good news of His ensuing mercy and We send down pure water from the sky.
notes: Linuhyiya bihi baldatan maytan wanusqiyahu mimma khalaqna anAAaman waanasiyya katheeran

arabic: لِّنُحۡـِۧيَ بِهِۦ بَلۡدَةٗ مَّيۡتٗا وَنُسۡقِيَهُۥ مِمَّا خَلَقۡنَآ أَنۡعَٰمٗا وَأَنَاسِيَّ كَثِيرٗا
transliteration: Linuhyiya bihi baldatan maytan wanusqiyahu mimma khalaqna anAAaman waanasiyya katheeran
translation: We send down water so that We may bring life to a dead land and slake the thirst aplenty of Our creations of cattle and mankind.
notes: Walaqad sarrafnahu baynahum liyaththakkaroo faaba aktharu alnnasi illa kufooran

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ صَرَّفۡنَٰهُ بَيۡنَهُمۡ لِيَذَّكَّرُواْ فَأَبَىٰٓ أَكۡثَرُ ٱلنَّاسِ إِلَّا كُفُورٗا
transliteration: Walaqad sarrafnahu baynahum liyaththakkaroo faaba aktharu alnnasi illa kufooran
translation: And We do distribute it (rainfall) among them so that they are gratefully aware thereof, but most of mankind respond not but in ingratitude.
notes: Walaw shi/na labaAAathna fee kulli qaryatin natheeran

arabic: وَلَوۡ شِئۡنَا لَبَعَثۡنَا فِي كُلِّ قَرۡيَةٖ نَّذِيرٗا
transliteration: Walaw shina labaAAathna fee kulli qaryatin natheeran
translation: And if We had so willed, We would have certainly raised someone to warn, in every town/village.
notes: Fala tutiAAi alkafireena wajahidhum bihi jihadan kabeeran

arabic: فَلَا تُطِعِ ٱلۡكَٰفِرِينَ وَجَٰهِدۡهُم بِهِۦ جِهَادٗا كَبِيرٗا
transliteration: Fala tutiAAi alkafireena wajahidhum bihi jihadan kabeeran
translation: Do not yield then to those who suppress the Truth, but counter them strenuously by means of this [Qur’aan].
notes: Wahuwa allathee maraja albahrayni hatha AAathbun furatun wahatha milhun ojajun wajaAAala baynahuma barzakhan wahijran mahjooran

arabic: ۞وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي مَرَجَ ٱلۡبَحۡرَيۡنِ هَٰذَا عَذۡبٞ فُرَاتٞ وَهَٰذَا مِلۡحٌ أُجَاجٞ وَجَعَلَ بَيۡنَهُمَا بَرۡزَخٗا وَحِجۡرٗا مَّحۡجُورٗا
transliteration: Wahuwa allathee maraja albahrayni hatha AAathbun furatun wahatha milhun ojajun wajaAAala baynahuma barzakhan wahijran mahjooran
translation: It is He who has facilitated two seas to flow and meet – one palatable and sweet and the other salty and bitter – and set up a barrier and an invincible partition between them.
notes:
At the mouth of River Amazon (South America) the ocean has fresh water extending so far out thereinto that the land cannot be seen from there. The ocean beyond this fresh water area is salty. This Verse here obviously refers to such huge bodies of water meeting each other without influenceing the character of either. This is the only place where such a thing spectacularly happens anywhere in the world. And at the time when the Qur’aan was revealed, the Americas were unknown to the rest of the world. And yet it is mentioned in the Qur’aan. This provides yet another proof that the Book is divine. Following is the relevant extract from Wikipedia: The quantity of water released by the Amazon to the Atlantic Ocean is enormous: up to 300,000 cubic metres per second (11,000,000 cu ft/s) in the rainy season, with an average of 209,000 cubic metres per second (7,400,000 cu ft/s) from 1973 to 1990. The Amazon is responsible for about 20% of the Earth's fresh water entering the ocean. The river pushes a vast plume of fresh water into the ocean. The plume is about 400 kilometres (250 mi) long and between 100 and 200 kilometres (62 and 120 mi) wide. The fresh water, being lighter, overrides the salty ocean, diluting the salinity and altering the color of the ocean surface over an area up to 1,000,000 square miles (2,600,000 km2) large. For centuries ships have reported fresh water near the Amazon's mouth yet well out of sight of land in what otherwise seemed to be the open ocean. [Source]

Wahuwa allathee khalaqa mina alma-i basharan fajaAAalahu nasaban wasihran wakana rabbuka qadeeran

arabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي خَلَقَ مِنَ ٱلۡمَآءِ بَشَرٗا فَجَعَلَهُۥ نَسَبٗا وَصِهۡرٗاۗ وَكَانَ رَبُّكَ قَدِيرٗا
transliteration: Wahuwa allathee khalaqa mina almai basharan fajaAAalahu nasaban wasihran wakana rabbuka qadeeran
translation: And He it is who has created a human being from water and then made him a link in the human relationships by blood and marriage. And your Lord is all powerful.
notes: WayaAAbudoona min dooni Allahi ma la yanfaAAuhum wala yadurruhum wakana alkafiru AAala rabbihi thaheeran

arabic: وَيَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنفَعُهُمۡ وَلَا يَضُرُّهُمۡۗ وَكَانَ ٱلۡكَافِرُ عَلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ ظَهِيرٗا
transliteration: WayaAAbudoona min dooni Allahi ma la yanfaAAuhum wala yadurruhum wakana alkafiru AAala rabbihi thaheeran
translation: Yet they worship besides Allah others that can neither benefit nor harm them. And the suppressor of the Truth does ever turn his back on his Lord.
notes: Wama arsalnaka illa mubashshiran wanatheeran

arabic: وَمَآ أَرۡسَلۡنَٰكَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّرٗا وَنَذِيرٗا
transliteration: Wama arsalnaka illa mubashshiran wanatheeran
translation: And We have not sent you (Prophet Muhammad) but to give good news to those who believe in Allah and abide by His Guidance given in the Qur’aan and to warn those who believe not.
notes: Qul ma as-alukum AAalayhi min ajrin illa man shaa an yattakhitha ila rabbihi sabeelan

arabic: قُلۡ مَآ أَسۡـَٔلُكُمۡ عَلَيۡهِ مِنۡ أَجۡرٍ إِلَّا مَن شَآءَ أَن يَتَّخِذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ سَبِيلٗا
transliteration: Qul ma asalukum AAalayhi min ajrin illa man shaa an yattakhitha ila rabbihi sabeelan
translation: Say, “I do not ask you for anything in return for this except that anyone who so wishes should take the Right Path to his Lord.”
notes: Watawakkal AAala alhayyi allathee la yamootu wasabbih bihamdihi wakafa bihi bithunoobi AAibadihi khabeeran

arabic: وَتَوَكَّلۡ عَلَى ٱلۡحَيِّ ٱلَّذِي لَا يَمُوتُ وَسَبِّحۡ بِحَمۡدِهِۦۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِهِۦ بِذُنُوبِ عِبَادِهِۦ خَبِيرًا
transliteration: Watawakkal AAala alhayyi allathee la yamootu wasabbih bihamdihi wakafa bihi bithunoobi AAibadihi khabeeran
translation: And have trust on the Living [Allah] Who never dies and glorify Him with His praise. And He is well conversant with the sins His subjects commit.
notes: Allathee khalaqa alssamawati waal-arda wama baynahuma fee sittati ayyamin thumma istawa AAala alAAarshi alrrahmanu fais-al bihi khabeeran

arabic: ٱلَّذِي خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٖ ثُمَّ ٱسۡتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلۡعَرۡشِۖ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ فَسۡـَٔلۡ بِهِۦ خَبِيرٗا
transliteration: Allathee khalaqa alssamawati waalarda wama baynahuma fee sittati ayyamin thumma istawa AAala alAAarshi alrrahmanu faisal bihi khabeeran
translation: It is He who created the heavens and the earth, and all besides that is in and around them, in six periods of time. HE then ascended the Throne of absolute power and authority. HE is the Gracious One. Do ask any informed person about Him.
notes: Wa-itha qeela lahumu osjudoo lilrrahmani qaloo wama alrrahmanu anasjudu lima ta/muruna wazadahum nufooran

arabic: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ ٱسۡجُدُواْۤ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِ قَالُواْ وَمَا ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ أَنَسۡجُدُ لِمَا تَأۡمُرُنَا وَزَادَهُمۡ نُفُورٗا۩
transliteration: Waitha qeela lahumu osjudoo lilrrahmani qaloo wama alrrahmanu anasjudu lima tamuruna wazadahum nufooran
translation: When they are told to prostrate before the Gracious One, they ask, “Who is this Gracious One? Are we to prostrate to whatever you order us?” And it increases their aversion.
notes: Tabaraka allathee jaAAala fee alssama-i buroojan wajaAAala feeha sirajan waqamaran muneeran

arabic: تَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِي جَعَلَ فِي ٱلسَّمَآءِ بُرُوجٗا وَجَعَلَ فِيهَا سِرَٰجٗا وَقَمَرٗا مُّنِيرٗا
transliteration: Tabaraka allathee jaAAala fee alssamai buroojan wajaAAala feeha sirajan waqamaran muneeran
translation: He is the bestower of blessings who has set constellations in the heavens, as also a lamp (sun) and a moon that give light (for the inhabitants of this earth).
notes: Wahuwa allathee jaAAala allayla waalnnahara khilfatan liman arada an yaththakkara aw arada shukooran

arabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي جَعَلَ ٱلَّيۡلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ خِلۡفَةٗ لِّمَنۡ أَرَادَ أَن يَذَّكَّرَ أَوۡ أَرَادَ شُكُورٗا
transliteration: Wahuwa allathee jaAAala allayla waalnnahara khilfatan liman arada an yaththakkara aw arada shukooran
translation: And He it is who has made the night and the day succeed each other – for the one who would ponder or be grateful.
notes:
There is a distinct divine prompting here for the believers to ponder over the natural phenomenon of the day and the night. Some Muslims with a scientific bend of mind did ponder and were successful in discovering that the earth was not stationary as people believed till then. Their pioneering discoveries led to the discovery of the real cause behind the occurrence of day and night. [Source] And for the Muslims in general the phenomenon is something to be very grateful about to their Lord; for, without it, it would be impossible to live on earth.

WaAAibadu alrrahmani allatheena yamshoona AAala al-ardi hawnan wa-itha khatabahumu aljahiloona qaloo salaman

arabic: وَعِبَادُ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ ٱلَّذِينَ يَمۡشُونَ عَلَى ٱلۡأَرۡضِ هَوۡنٗا وَإِذَا خَاطَبَهُمُ ٱلۡجَٰهِلُونَ قَالُواْ سَلَٰمٗا
transliteration: WaAAibadu alrrahmani allatheena yamshoona AAala alardi hawnan waitha khatabahumu aljahiloona qaloo salaman
translation: And human beings obedient to the Gracious One are those who walk on earth with humility and when the ignorant speak to them, they say, “Peace.”
notes: Waallatheena yabeetoona lirabbihim sujjadan waqiyaman

arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ يَبِيتُونَ لِرَبِّهِمۡ سُجَّدٗا وَقِيَٰمٗا
transliteration: Waallatheena yabeetoona lirabbihim sujjadan waqiyaman
translation: And who spend the night for their Lord, lying down and standing.
notes:
My understanding of this Verse is that the Lord wants the righteous people not to do anything wrong, improper, unlawful or illegal under cover of darkness of the night. HE wants no wrong done during the day too, but the night provides more temptations for wrong-doing, and hence the emphasis thereon.

Waallatheena yaqooloona rabbana isrif AAanna AAathaba jahannama inna AAathabaha kana gharaman

arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا ٱصۡرِفۡ عَنَّا عَذَابَ جَهَنَّمَۖ إِنَّ عَذَابَهَا كَانَ غَرَامًا
transliteration: Waallatheena yaqooloona rabbana isrif AAanna AAathaba jahannama inna AAathabaha kana gharaman
translation: And who say, “Our Lord! Ward off from us the punishment of Hell. Its punishment is indeed atrocious.”
notes: Innaha saat mustaqarran wamuqaman

arabic: إِنَّهَا سَآءَتۡ مُسۡتَقَرّٗا وَمُقَامٗا
transliteration: Innaha saat mustaqarran wamuqaman
translation: Indeed, it is an evil abode and an evil position to be in.
notes: Waallatheena itha anfaqoo lam yusrifoo walam yaqturoo wakana bayna thalika qawaman

arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ إِذَآ أَنفَقُواْ لَمۡ يُسۡرِفُواْ وَلَمۡ يَقۡتُرُواْ وَكَانَ بَيۡنَ ذَٰلِكَ قَوَامٗا
transliteration: Waallatheena itha anfaqoo lam yusrifoo walam yaqturoo wakana bayna thalika qawaman
translation: And they (human beings obedient to the Gracious One) are those who are neither spendthrift nor miserly but keep to a position in between.
notes: Waallatheena la yadAAoona maAAa Allahi ilahan akhara wala yaqtuloona alnnafsa allatee harrama Allahu illa bialhaqqi wala yaznoona waman yafAAal thalika yalqa athaman

arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَدۡعُونَ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا ءَاخَرَ وَلَا يَقۡتُلُونَ ٱلنَّفۡسَ ٱلَّتِي حَرَّمَ ٱللَّهُ إِلَّا بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَلَا يَزۡنُونَۚ وَمَن يَفۡعَلۡ ذَٰلِكَ يَلۡقَ أَثَامٗا
transliteration: Waallatheena la yadAAoona maAAa Allahi ilahan akhara wala yaqtuloona alnnafsa allatee harrama Allahu illa bialhaqqi wala yaznoona waman yafAAal thalika yalqa athaman
translation: And who invoke no deity besides Allah, nor kill anyone whose killing, without due authority, Allah has prohibited, nor indulge in extramarital sexual intercourse. And whoever does that, he/she commits a sin.
notes: YudaAAaf lahu alAAathabu yawma alqiyamati wayakhlud feehi muhanan

arabic: يُضَٰعَفۡ لَهُ ٱلۡعَذَابُ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ وَيَخۡلُدۡ فِيهِۦ مُهَانًا
transliteration: YudaAAaf lahu alAAathabu yawma alqiyamati wayakhlud feehi muhanan
translation: He/she shall have his/her suffering doubled on the Day of Resurrection and shall remain humiliated therein forever.
notes: Illa man taba waamana waAAamila AAamalan salihan faola-ika yubaddilu Allahu sayyi-atihim hasanatin wakana Allahu ghafooran raheeman

arabic: إِلَّا مَن تَابَ وَءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ عَمَلٗا صَٰلِحٗا فَأُوْلَـٰٓئِكَ يُبَدِّلُ ٱللَّهُ سَيِّـَٔاتِهِمۡ حَسَنَٰتٖۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورٗا رَّحِيمٗا
transliteration: Illa man taba waamana waAAamila AAamalan salihan faolaika yubaddilu Allahu sayyiatihim hasanatin wakana Allahu ghafooran raheeman
translation: But for those who repent, believe, and do good deeds, Allah will change their evil deeds into good ones. And Allah is forgiving, merciful.
notes: Waman taba waAAamila salihan fa-innahu yatoobu ila Allahi mataban

arabic: وَمَن تَابَ وَعَمِلَ صَٰلِحٗا فَإِنَّهُۥ يَتُوبُ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ مَتَابٗا
transliteration: Waman taba waAAamila salihan fainnahu yatoobu ila Allahi mataban
translation: And the one who repents and reforms, he/she then has indeed turned to Allah in repentance.
notes: Waallatheena la yashhadoona alzzoora wa-itha marroo biallaghwi marroo kiraman

arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَشۡهَدُونَ ٱلزُّورَ وَإِذَا مَرُّواْ بِٱللَّغۡوِ مَرُّواْ كِرَامٗا
transliteration: Waallatheena la yashhadoona alzzoora waitha marroo biallaghwi marroo kiraman
translation: And they (human beings obedient to the Gracious One) are those who do not give false evidence, and when they pass by frivolity, they pass by with dignity.
notes: Waallatheena itha thukkiroo bi-ayati rabbihim lam yakhirroo AAalayha summan waAAumyanan

arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِّرُواْ بِـَٔايَٰتِ رَبِّهِمۡ لَمۡ يَخِرُّواْ عَلَيۡهَا صُمّٗا وَعُمۡيَانٗا
transliteration: Waallatheena itha thukkiroo biayati rabbihim lam yakhirroo AAalayha summan waAAumyanan
translation: And who, when reminded of the Verses/signs of their Lord, do not stumble upon them, deaf and blind.
notes: Waallatheena yaqooloona rabbana hab lana min azwajina wathurriyyatina qurrata aAAyunin waijAAalna lilmuttaqeena imaman

arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا هَبۡ لَنَا مِنۡ أَزۡوَٰجِنَا وَذُرِّيَّـٰتِنَا قُرَّةَ أَعۡيُنٖ وَٱجۡعَلۡنَا لِلۡمُتَّقِينَ إِمَامًا
transliteration: Waallatheena yaqooloona rabbana hab lana min azwajina wathurriyyatina qurrata aAAyunin waijAAalna lilmuttaqeena imaman
translation: And who say, “Our Lord! Give us joy in our wives and our children and make us exemplary for the righteous.”
notes: Ola-ika yujzawna alghurfata bima sabaroo wayulaqqawna feeha tahiyyatan wasalaman

arabic: أُوْلَـٰٓئِكَ يُجۡزَوۡنَ ٱلۡغُرۡفَةَ بِمَا صَبَرُواْ وَيُلَقَّوۡنَ فِيهَا تَحِيَّةٗ وَسَلَٰمًا
transliteration: Olaika yujzawna alghurfata bima sabaroo wayulaqqawna feeha tahiyyatan wasalaman
translation: These are the ones who will be rewarded with high position because of their endurance. They will be welcomed therein with greetings and salutations of peace.
notes: Khalideena feeha hasunat mustaqarran wamuqaman

arabic: خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَاۚ حَسُنَتۡ مُسۡتَقَرّٗا وَمُقَامٗا
transliteration: Khalideena feeha hasunat mustaqarran wamuqaman
translation: They shall live there forever – the best place to reside in and the best position to be in.
notes: Qul ma yaAAbao bikum rabbee lawla duAAaokum faqad kaththabtum fasawfa yakoonu lizaman

arabic: قُلۡ مَا يَعۡبَؤُاْ بِكُمۡ رَبِّي لَوۡلَا دُعَآؤُكُمۡۖ فَقَدۡ كَذَّبۡتُمۡ فَسَوۡفَ يَكُونُ لِزَامَۢا
transliteration: Qul ma yaAAbao bikum rabbee lawla duAAaokum faqad kaththabtum fasawfa yakoonu lizaman
translation: Say, “Why would my Lord care for you, if you do not pray to and invoke Him? Verily then you have rejected the Truth.
